item.title: Using CVE-2023-41772 against kernel mode protections
item.description: Weaponizing a CVE Against Anti-Cheats So I've been working on this for a while now and I finally got the full chain working. The idea is simple, anti-cheats use ObRegisterCallbacks to strip your handles when you try to open their process. The exploit leverages CVE-2023-41772 with its POC that triggers code execution inside PPL-WinTcb WerFaultSecure.exe to get an all access handle on protected games. If we can get code running inside csrss, we can OpenProcess(PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS) on any process below the chain and the anti-cheat's kernel callbacks just have to let it go because disallowing csrss is looking for trouble.

Weaponizing a CVE Against Anti-Cheats So I've been working on this for a while now and I finally got the full chain working. The idea is simple, anti-cheats use ObRegisterCallbacks to strip your handles when you try to open their process. The exploit leverages CVE-2023-41772 with its POC that triggers code execution inside PPL-WinTcb WerFaultSecure.exe to get an all access handle on protected games. If we can get code running inside csrss, we can OpenProcess(PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS) on any process below the chain and the anti-cheat's kernel callbacks just have to let it go because disallowing csrss is looking for trouble.
